heading: Transportation research and development 5-year strategic plan
label: 6503 Transportation research and development 5-year strategic plan
notes: Amendments2021—Subsec. (a). , substituted “Not later than 180 days after the date of publication of the Department of Transportation Strategic Plan and not less frequently than once every 5 years thereafter, the Secretary” for “The Secretary”.Pub. L. 117–58, § 25014(1)Subsec. (b). , substituted “Each strategic” for “The strategic” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 117–58, § 25014(2)Subsec. (c). , substituted “Each strategic” for “The strategic” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 117–58, § 25014(3)(A)Subsec. (c)(1)(G). , added subpar. (G).Pub. L. 117–58, § 25014(3)(B)Subsec. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Not later than 2 ½ years after the date of enactment of this chapter, the Secretary may publish on a public website an interim report that—Pub. L. 117–58, § 25014(5)“(1) provides an assessment of the 5-year research and development strategic plan of the Department of Transportation described in this section; and“(2) includes a description of the extent to which the research and development is or is not successfully meeting the purposes described under subsection (c)(1).”

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of publication of the Department of Transportation Strategic Plan and not less frequently than once every 5 years thereafter, the Secretary shall develop a 5-year transportation research and development strategic plan to guide future Federal transportation research and development activities.
;section 306 of title 5
sections 1115 and 1116 of title 31; and
any other research and development plan within the Department of Transportation.

children.label: A
children.text: improving mobility of people and goods;

children.label: B
children.text: reducing congestion;

children.label: C
children.text: promoting safety;

children.label: D
children.text: improving the durability and extending the life of transportation infrastructure;

children.label: E
children.text: preserving the environment;

children.label: F
children.text: preserving the existing transportation system; and

children.label: G
children.text: reducing transportation cybersecurity risks;

children.label: A
children.text: fundamental research pertaining to the applied physical and natural sciences;

children.label: B
children.text: applied science and research;

children.label: C
children.text: technology development research; and

children.label: D
children.text: social science research; and

children.label: A
children.text: identify the anticipated annual funding levels for the period covered by the strategic plan; and

children.label: B
children.text: describe the research findings the Department expects to discover at the end of the period covered by the strategic plan.
reflects input from a wide range of external stakeholders;

includes and integrates the research and development programs of all of the modal administrations of the Department of Transportation, including aviation, transit, rail, and maritime and joint programs;
takes into account research and development by other Federal, State, local, private sector, and nonprofit institutions;
not later than , and not less frequently than once every 5 years thereafter, is published on a public website; andDecember 31, 2021

children.label: A
children.text: contributes to the achievement of the purposes identified under subsection (c)(1); and

children.label: B
children.text: avoids unnecessary duplication of those efforts.
